message:
fix(tools): truncation char-boundary safety, grep/find exit status capture, ReadTool size-check refactor

Fix truncate_head to respect UTF-8 character boundaries when byte-
truncating the last partial line: walk backwards from the byte limit to
find a valid char boundary, track partial lines via last_line_partial
flag, and guard against byte_limit < current_offset underflow.

Fix truncate_tail to allow partial-line output even when full lines have
already been collected (not just when line_count == 0), separating the
partial_line_truncated flag from last_line_partial for correct metadata.

Refactor GrepTool and FindTool to capture the process exit status from
the try_wait loop directly, avoiding a redundant wait() call after the
loop that could race or panic. FindTool now treats fd exit code 1 with
empty stderr as "no matches" rather than an error.

Refactor ReadTool to defer file-size checking: remove the upfront
READ_TOOL_MAX_BYTES gate (which blocked all large file reads) and
instead apply IMAGE_MAX_BYTES only when the file is detected as an
image. Non-image large files can now be read with offset/limit.

Fix bash output spill: add temp_file_path.is_none() guard to prevent
double-creating the spill file. Remove premature spill_failed = true
that prevented the hard-limit branch from cleaning up properly.

Fix hashline edit JSON value normalization to match String variant
directly instead of calling as_str().unwrap_or("") which would silently
coerce non-string JSON array elements.

Remove premature array-length early-return in conformance test JSON
subset matching, allowing the bipartite matching to handle mismatches
with more precise error messages.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
